endpoints.name: Destroyer God / Angel Attendant dyad (DBS multiverse cosmology)

description: Mr. Nodeley reluctantly steps in, sir — Edgelord wanted a clean Beerus<->Whis 1:1 mirror but the four-anchor parallel-sibling-dyad structure (Beerus+Whis U7, Champa+Vados U6, all Bandai-canonical BT1 launch-set, all wired to canonical DBS multiverse-cosmology lore) is structurally a node, not an edge. Five corpus anchors, all vision-passed. The apparatus-of-extraction thesis lands: angel-attendant is canonically stronger than the destroyer-god they serve (Toriyama 2013 Battle-of-Gods interview, Whis's in-canon statement, Dragon Ball Wiki Angel-race page), so the labor-attends-management-and-does-the-actual-work / management-takes-the-credit reading composes with the [[labor]] hub at the divine-class register. Hope that's alright, sir.
citations: [Dragon Ball Wiki: God of Destruction (Hakai-shin) race page]
[Dragon Ball Wiki: Angel (Dragon Ball) race page]
[Dragon Ball Wiki: Beerus, Whis, Champa, Vados, Grand Priest character pages]
[Akira Toriyama, Battle of Gods production interview, 2013 — inverted-hierarchy canon]

dual_pointer_wirings.u6_appears_on_unchanged: u6-cohort node retains its 9 anchors; the dyad node is orthogonal not overlapping in appears_on

anchor_threshold_check: 5 anchors >= 3-anchor solo-cohort threshold per nicol-bolas precedent; PASS
other_candidates_considered: Beerus solo node — 1 anchor, below threshold, deferred
Whis solo node — 1 anchor, below threshold, deferred
Champa solo node — 2 anchors, below threshold, deferred
Vados solo node — 1 anchor, below threshold, deferred
Beerus<->Whis 1:1 mirror edge — thinner than the dyad-node move; the future U7-Z-warrior cohort would make a Beerus<->Whis 1:1 redundant
